step_rationale: Dihydroepoxyeicosatrienoic acid; EET metabolite increased post-PCI, marking eicosanoid turnover and vascular healing.
mechanism: DHETs, the hydrolyzed metabolites of EETs, accumulate post-PCI and mark active eicosanoid-mediated vascular repair and endothelial recovery.

references.title: Metabolomics reveal dynamic changes in eicosanoid profile in patients with ST-elevation myocardial infarction after percutaneous coronary intervention.
